page: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 12 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon Cascadelake processor. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the system provides 12 physical cores with a single thread per core operating at 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared. It is equipped with 24.0 GB of system memory, translating to 2.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is bundled with a 500 GB local NVMe SSD, and the server includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No GPU accelerators are present.

Benchmark results place this server consistently in the average performance tier across most standardized tests. The stress-ng single-core CPU performance is recorded at 1418.58, while multi-core performance reaches 17309.27. Memory bandwidth tests show 1224.9 GB/sec for small-block cached reads, dropping to 87.74 GB/sec for large-block uncached reads and 47.62 GB/sec for large-block writes. Cryptographic and vector processing capabilities are supported by AVX, AVX512F, and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets, yielding a Passmark CPU extended instructions score of 18427.62 and an encryption test score of 6545.88.

The server exhibits a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, which aligns it with compute-heavy profiles rather than memory-intensive configurations. While it handles small-scale language model tasks adequately, achieving 719.57 tokens/sec for prompt processing on a 135M model, performance drops significantly on larger workloads. For a medium 7B model, prompt processing falls to a weak 16.55 tokens/sec and text generation to 7.43 tokens/sec. The shared CPU allocation also introduces potential resource contention tradeoffs. However, with 18.53 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and 11.99 GB/sec static web serving throughput, the server is well-suited for web serving, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression tasks.
description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server configured with 12 shared vCPUs on an Intel Xeon Cascadelake x86_64 architecture. Operating at 2.0 GHz with 12 physical cores and one thread per core, the server is paired with 24.0 GB of RAM and a 500 GB local NVMe SSD. Benchmark data indicates average tier performance, with a Passmark CPU score of 17934.88 and a multi-core stress-ng score of 17309.27. The 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes it a cost-efficient option for compute-focused workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. However, the shared CPU allocation and weak performance on medium LLM inference tasks represent notable tradeoffs. It is best suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and general-purpose multi-threaded applications.
og_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance virtual server featuring 12 shared Intel Xeon cores, 24 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD, delivering balanced compute and storage for web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vhp-12c-24gb-intel is a High Performance server with 12 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 24 GB RAM, and a 500 GB NVMe SSD for compute-focused workloads.
tagline: A compute-focused virtual server featuring twelve shared Intel Xeon cores, integrated NVMe SSD storage, and balanced multi-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: 12 shared Intel Xeon Cascadelake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
24.0 GB of system memory and 500 GB local NVMe SSD storage
AVX-512 instruction support for enhanced vector and cryptographic operations
Average-tier multi-core performance with 17309.27 stress-ng score
Best suited for static web serving, Redis databases, and multi-threaded decompression
